title: Shadow puppet papercutting
materials: black card stock 220 gsm (or cartridge-paper-220 painted black)
bamboo skewers or drinking straws for handles
sources: Shadow puppet tradition documented in various public domain sources including: Théodore Pavie, 'Etudes sur l'Inde' (1848) — public domain (French, describes shadow theatre). Southeast Asian Wayang Kulit tradition (PD by age). Focus on the paper-cutting craft of the silhouette shape.
notes: Cutting a jointed or single-piece shadow puppet from black card stock. The tutorial focuses on the paper-cut craft: silhouette design, cutting method, and handle attachment. NOT about shadow theatre performance. Covers: designing a profile figure (profile views show the character best for shadows — full face doesn't read), cutting the silhouette starting with the interior negative space, cutting the outer edge last, leaving a solid area for handle attachment, attaching a skewer handle with PVA. Optional: two-piece jointed limb (the simplest articulation — one pivoted arm held with a brass paper fastener). Traditional decorative perforations: the interior openings in traditional Wayang-Kulit shadow puppets are created with a bookbinding awl.
